subject: English
skill: Story Elements, Theme, and Character Analysis

lesson.story_mission: Enter Story Evidence Theater, where every reader maps characters, setting, plot, point of view, and theme before making a claim.
lesson.objective: I can identify story elements, theme, point of view, and character responses using evidence from the text.
lesson.concept_explanation: Literary readers track who is in the story, where and when it happens, what problem changes the plot, how characters respond, and what lesson the whole story suggests. A theme is bigger than one detail, and a character trait must be supported by actions, words, or thoughts.
lesson.common_misconception: A reader may confuse a character trait with a feeling, mix up point of view, choose a tiny detail as the theme, or put events out of order.
lesson.real_world_connection: Story analysis helps you discuss novels, write reading responses, and explain why a character changes.
lesson.lesson_summary: Map the story, study character evidence, order key events, and choose a theme that fits the whole passage.
lesson.mini_lesson: First map characters and setting. Then follow the problem and solution. Finally, use evidence to explain traits, point of view, and theme.

misconception_bank.character_trait_confusion: The answer names a mood or detail instead of a stable trait supported by evidence.
misconception_bank.point_of_view_confusion: The answer mixes up who is telling the story or how the narrator knows events.
misconception_bank.theme_detail_confusion: The answer chooses one small event instead of the larger lesson.
misconception_bank.sequence_order_error: The answer places plot events in an order the passage does not support.

story.text: Listen to this page. In this mission, you enter a Grade 4 reading studio for analyzing story elements, theme, point of view, and character response. You will learn why the skill matters, watch a model, practice with feedback, and then prove your strategy at checkpoint. Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This mini lesson teaches analyzing story elements, theme, point of view, and character response. First, name the reading job. Next, use the visual model to notice important words and clues. Finally, answer in a way that matches the text instead of guessing.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: This Watch page models the strategy. Listen for the teacher thinking aloud, because strong readers explain what they notice before they answer. Watch how the clue is connected to the correct answer.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This demo gives another guided example. Read the passage, point to the clue, and decide which answer is best supported. The goal is careful reading, not just speed.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: Practice time. Read the item, use the visual model, and press Read Question if you want the exact prompt read aloud. Press Listen when hearing the story, passage, or sentence will help. Choose or write an answer that fits the text.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: Challenge time. These questions ask you to apply analyzing story elements, theme, point of view, and character response with less help. Press Read Question for narration, and use Listen when audio helps you follow the reading. Slow down if two answers seem close.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: Checkpoint time. Show that you can use analyzing story elements, theme, point of view, and character response independently. Press Read Question if you want the prompt read aloud, and press Listen when audio will help you hear the passage. Answer carefully, then review your feedback.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: This badge page celebrates your reading progress. You earned credit for using evidence, models, and careful thinking. Keep using this strategy whenever you meet a challenging text.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This profile page shows your growth and next steps. Review your accuracy, hints, and level progress. If one level needs more work, choose Practice This Skill and return to that level in the Skill Practice Center.
profile.label: Read This Page
